title: rich crumbly french veal casseroles
description: a rich and flavorful french dish featuring veal, topped with a sticky glaze made from beef broth and butter. ideal for a dinner party or special occasion.

ingredients.name: low-glycemic sugar
ingredients.notes: such as honey or maple syrup.

instructions: preheat the oven to 400 degrees fahrenheit
season the veal cutlets with salt and pepper, then dust with flour
heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. add the veal cutlets and cook until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side, then transfer to a baking dish.

in a small saucepan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. add the beef broth, mustard, and sugar. bring to a simmer and cook until the glaze has thickened, stirring occasionally.
roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. place the pastry over the veal cutlets, pressing gently to adhere.
cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape. brush the top of the pastry with the egg wash and sprinkle with gruyère cheese, if using.
place the baking dish in the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the veal is cooked through.
remove the dish from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. garnish with fresh parsley and thyme.
